retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

- Chatbot: General chat function with image analysis and internet search capabilities
- Image generator: Tool for generating images from text descriptions
- Translator: Translating text and files in the same layout
- Document analysis: Tool for analyzing written content from documents and option for Internet research
- Laboratory: Experimental environment for testing functions and models

#

| Chatbot | Document analysis | Laboratory | Image generator | Translator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upload images and screenshots in chat to chat about image content | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files | No uploading of documents | Prompt limited to 4000 characters Created images cannot be customized by entering additional descriptions | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (max 40 MB per file, max 250 MB total) |
| Internet research | Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research |
| No uploading of documents | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of reference images | No uploading of images |

heading_path: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
retrieved.text:
u:ai also offers an important advantage in terms of copyright. Since uploaded content is not used for AI training, you can upload copyrighted material to u:ai (but please note the regulations on [private copying](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/#c1268388)).

With commercial tools, uploads can be incorporated into training data or further processed. This means that users lose control over how their content is used. Therefore, you may only use copyrighted material in commercial tools if the data is not used for training AI tools.

In summary, the following applies:

u:ai offers a clearly regulated, transparent, and legally compliant framework for the use of AI in academic studies. This makes the platform particularly suitable for university work processes where data protection, copyright, and scientific integrity play a central role.

retrieved.title: Using AI in Your Studies
retrieved.text: Imagine you are tasked with writing an academic paper. You are aware of the importance of a clear research question, thorough research and a coherent line of argument. Yet instead of doing everything yourself, there is a new option: AI-supported tools can suggest literature, generate outlines, draft opening sentences and improve your writing style. Artificial intelligence has long become part of everyday student life. At the same time, its use in degree programmes raises important questions: What is permitted? Where are the boundaries? And what do students need to be aware of? On these pages, you will find concise and reliable information on the responsible use of AI in degree programmes at the University of Vienna. You will learn under which conditions AI may be used, which specific provisions apply to examinations and what requires particular attention when using AI. The content is based on the current regulations of the University of Vienna and is intended for students of all disciplines. The aim is to support a responsible, transparent and reflective use of AI. retrieved.title: AI in academic writing
heading_path: Use Cases of AI in scientific writing
retrieved.text: In the context of academic writing, it is the responsibility of the lecturer to determine the permitted use of aids. Any tools that are not explicitly permitted are prohibited. Prior to commencing work on your text, it is essential to familiarise yourself with the documentation requirements for the use of text-generating AI and other aids. It is imperative to take [data protection considerations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/#c1269046) into account at all times.

retrieved.title: Basic framework for AI use
retrieved.text:
The use of AI in degree programmes is not generally prohibited, but nor is it permitted without any restrictions. Whether and how you may use AI always depends on the specific context, such as:

- the course type
- the examination or assessment format
- the specific requirements set by the lecturer
- the respective learning objectives
- the [principles of good academic practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035)

As a general rule, you are responsible for all work that you submit, including any that has been created using AI tools. This implies that:

- You are responsible for the accuracy, quality and traceability of your work.
- Any incorrect statements, distortions or fabricated sources are your responsibility.
- While AI may support you in your work, the substantive contribution and independent thinking must be your own.

retrieved.title: Checklist for the responsible use of AI
heading_path: Checklist for the responsible use of AI
retrieved.text: General information - Do not use generative AI for research - Do not trust AI-generated content; check everything - Write specific prompts with a clear task, context, and details on format and style - Do not rely on AI-generated summaries - Process AI output further - For each course, check the information in u:find, on Moodle, and information on specific assignments and examinations, and clarify any open questions about the use of AI with the respective lecturer Privacy Policy & Terms of Use - Do not enter sensitive or confidential data into AI tools - Anonymise or avoid entering personal data. - Obtain the express consent of third parties before processing their data in an AI. - Ensure that the data you enter is not used for AI training - Only use GDPR-compliant AI tools that do not store data on servers outside the EU. Copyright - Check whether AI-generated texts are based on copyrighted content and cite the authors appropriately - Ensure that copyrighted texts are reproduced correctly. - AI does not have copyright to its own output. Nevertheless, you may not pass off the content as your own. - Bear in mind that texts and documents you enter into AI may themselves be copyrighted: therefore, ensure that your input is not used for AI training. Honesty & Independence - Do not pass off AI-generated content as your own

heading_path: Examinations with AI: What does apply here?
retrieved.text: permitted is prohibited. If you are unsure, you should always consult your lecturers in order to clarify expectations and framework conditions at an early stage. - Transparency and documentation: If you are permitted to use AI tools for written assignments, you must [fully disclose](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/citing-and-labelling-ai/#c1269189) such use in accordance with the principles of good academic practice. - Examination formats are changing: You should be prepared for greater emphasis on competences such as reflection and transfer. To ensure the independence of your performance, written assignments may be supplemented by oral components, plausibility checks or grade-relevant discussions. - Personal responsibility: It is imperative that you, the author, accept full responsibility for the accuracy, academic validity and integrity of the results submitted. As language models are not "knowledge machines" and may produce [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268979), it is essential that all AI-generated content is critically reviewed. - Consequences of misconduct: The use of unauthorised AI tools constitutes academic misconduct. This results in a non-assessment (entry of an “X”) or the annulment of a grade that has already been awarded. In serious cases, the Rectorate may impose exclusion from studies for up to two semesters.

retrieved.title: Maintaining independence: AI-generated or AI-supported?
heading_path: Controlling AI in a targeted manner and maintaining independence
retrieved.text:
The quality of AI-generated output depends to a large extent on how precisely and reflectively a request is formulated. AI tools do not independently respond to academic requirements; rather, they orient themselves to the specifications they receive through [prompts](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/controlling-ai-in-a-targeted-manner-the-importance-of-prompting-correctly/).

It is important to define the objective, context and framework conditions clearly, as this will ensure the most effective use of AI as a supportive tool. Through targeted prompting, the user retains control over the working process, determines the direction and depth of responses, and ensures that substantive decisions remain theirs. AI thus provides impulses – the academic contextualisation and further development remain the responsibility of the user.

Formulieren Sie Prompts so, dass die KI **nicht für Sie denkt oder schreibt**, sondern Sie beim eigenen Denken unterstützt. Bitten Sie um **Fragen, Alternativen, Rückmeldungen oder Strukturvorschläge**, nicht um fertige Texte oder inhaltliche Entscheidungen. Bewährt haben sich Prompts, die:

- die KI auf eine **unterstützende Rolle** festlegen (zB Feedback, Reflexionshilfe),
- klar benennen, **was die KI nicht tun soll** (zB keine fertigen Argumente),
- deutlich machen, dass **Auswahl, Bewertung und Weiterverarbeitung** bei Ihnen liegen.

**Dabei gilt:** Die Vorschläge dienen als Ausgangspunkt und müssen fachlich geprüft, eingeordnet und weiterentwickelt werden.

retrieved.title: What is permitted? And what is not?
retrieved.text:
Whether and how you may use AI in a course or in an examination is determined by the learning outcomes, the fundamental [principles of good academic practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035) and the requirements set by the respective lecturer. Therefore, always check out

- the information in u:find
- notices on Moodle
- information on specific assignments and examinations

In the event of ambiguity surrounding the use of AI, it is advisable to consult with the lecturer in advance to establish clear regulations. In instances where there is uncertainty, it is prudent to assume that AI is not permitted unless explicit permission has been obtained.

AI may be used, provided that responsibility for the content is retained and the independent nature of the work is preserved. For example, AI may be used to:

- generate ideas or structure topics
- gain an overview of a new topic or prepare for reading
- revise the language of one's own texts
- support reflection or check comprehensibility
- find examples or alternatives which are reviewed and contextualised independently

The use of AI must always be compatible with the requirements set by the lecturer and must not replace your independent contribution.

retrieved.title: Further information
retrieved.text:
Further links with additional information, services and guidelines on dealing with artificial intelligence can be found here.

If you have any questions about the use of AI in courses, please always consult the following first:

- the information in u:find
- notes on Moodle and
- information on specific assignments and examinations

If anything is unclear, please always speak to the course teacher.

There are interdisciplinary courses on AI that are also eligible for ECTS:

- Extension Curriculum ["Understanding Artificial Intelligence"](https://informatik.univie.ac.at/studium/studienangebot/erweiterungscurricula-ec/kuenstliche-intelligenz-verstehen/)
- Courses on AI in [Studienstart Plus](https://studienstartplus.univie.ac.at/)

The [Centre for Teaching and Learning](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/) offers consultations and workshops on AI:

- **[Peer Point](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/student-research-hub/ctl-peer-point/)** for questions about using AI in teaching? Peer Point is here to help you.
- **[Handouts for Students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/ressourcen/handouts-leitfaeden-und-infomaterial/#c1123913)**
- [Schreib-](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/waehrend-des-studiums/schreibberatung/moegliche-themen-einer-schreibberatung/) und Lernberatungen ([PFSB](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/waehrend-des-studiums/peer-studienfortschrittsberatung/))

retrieved.title: AI in your Studies: What is permitted?
retrieved.text:
AI can provide support at various points throughout your degree programme, such as idea structuring, understanding complex content and text revision. However, there are clear guidelines in place regarding the application and extent to which AI can be utilised in degree programmes.

The University of Vienna supports the responsible use of artificial intelligence in degree programmes, provided that such use is transparent, complies with regulations, upholds the [principles of academic integrity](https://backend.univie.ac.at/?id=225768), and that it supports you in achieving the intended learning outcomes.

Whether and how the use of AI is permitted always depends on the specific context. In case of doubt, the following applies: AI is not permitted unless clear permission has been granted.

[### Basic framework](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/basic-framework/)

[### What is permitted? And what is not?](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/what-is-permitted-and-what-is-not/)

[### Data protection and copyright](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/)

[## Things AI is not recommended for](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/things-ai-is-not-recommended-for/)

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

u:ai also offers an important advantage in terms of copyright. Since uploaded content is not used for AI training, you can upload copyrighted material to u:ai (but please note the regulations on [private copying](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/#c1268388)).

With commercial tools, uploads can be incorporated into training data or further processed. This means that users lose control over how their content is used. Therefore, you may only use copyrighted material in commercial tools if the data is not used for training AI tools.

In summary, the following applies:

u:ai offers a clearly regulated, transparent, and legally compliant framework for the use of AI in academic studies. This makes the platform particularly suitable for university work processes where data protection, copyright, and scientific integrity play a central role.

reranked.text: Imagine you are tasked with writing an academic paper. You are aware of the importance of a clear research question, thorough research and a coherent line of argument. Yet instead of doing everything yourself, there is a new option: AI-supported tools can suggest literature, generate outlines, draft opening sentences and improve your writing style. Artificial intelligence has long become part of everyday student life. At the same time, its use in degree programmes raises important questions: What is permitted? Where are the boundaries? And what do students need to be aware of? On these pages, you will find concise and reliable information on the responsible use of AI in degree programmes at the University of Vienna. You will learn under which conditions AI may be used, which specific provisions apply to examinations and what requires particular attention when using AI. The content is based on the current regulations of the University of Vienna and is intended for students of all disciplines. The aim is to support a responsible, transparent and reflective use of AI. reranked.title: AI in academic writing
heading_path: Use Cases of AI in scientific writing
reranked.text: In the context of academic writing, it is the responsibility of the lecturer to determine the permitted use of aids. Any tools that are not explicitly permitted are prohibited. Prior to commencing work on your text, it is essential to familiarise yourself with the documentation requirements for the use of text-generating AI and other aids. It is imperative to take [data protection considerations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/#c1269046) into account at all times.

reranked.title: Basic framework for AI use
reranked.text:
The use of AI in degree programmes is not generally prohibited, but nor is it permitted without any restrictions. Whether and how you may use AI always depends on the specific context, such as:

- the course type
- the examination or assessment format
- the specific requirements set by the lecturer
- the respective learning objectives
- the [principles of good academic practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035)

As a general rule, you are responsible for all work that you submit, including any that has been created using AI tools. This implies that:

- You are responsible for the accuracy, quality and traceability of your work.
- Any incorrect statements, distortions or fabricated sources are your responsibility.
- While AI may support you in your work, the substantive contribution and independent thinking must be your own.

reranked.title: Checklist for the responsible use of AI
heading_path: Checklist for the responsible use of AI
reranked.text: General information - Do not use generative AI for research - Do not trust AI-generated content; check everything - Write specific prompts with a clear task, context, and details on format and style - Do not rely on AI-generated summaries - Process AI output further - For each course, check the information in u:find, on Moodle, and information on specific assignments and examinations, and clarify any open questions about the use of AI with the respective lecturer Privacy Policy & Terms of Use - Do not enter sensitive or confidential data into AI tools - Anonymise or avoid entering personal data. - Obtain the express consent of third parties before processing their data in an AI. - Ensure that the data you enter is not used for AI training - Only use GDPR-compliant AI tools that do not store data on servers outside the EU. Copyright - Check whether AI-generated texts are based on copyrighted content and cite the authors appropriately - Ensure that copyrighted texts are reproduced correctly. - AI does not have copyright to its own output. Nevertheless, you may not pass off the content as your own. - Bear in mind that texts and documents you enter into AI may themselves be copyrighted: therefore, ensure that your input is not used for AI training. Honesty & Independence - Do not pass off AI-generated content as your own

heading_path: Examinations with AI: What does apply here?
reranked.text: permitted is prohibited. If you are unsure, you should always consult your lecturers in order to clarify expectations and framework conditions at an early stage. - Transparency and documentation: If you are permitted to use AI tools for written assignments, you must [fully disclose](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/citing-and-labelling-ai/#c1269189) such use in accordance with the principles of good academic practice. - Examination formats are changing: You should be prepared for greater emphasis on competences such as reflection and transfer. To ensure the independence of your performance, written assignments may be supplemented by oral components, plausibility checks or grade-relevant discussions. - Personal responsibility: It is imperative that you, the author, accept full responsibility for the accuracy, academic validity and integrity of the results submitted. As language models are not "knowledge machines" and may produce [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268979), it is essential that all AI-generated content is critically reviewed. - Consequences of misconduct: The use of unauthorised AI tools constitutes academic misconduct. This results in a non-assessment (entry of an “X”) or the annulment of a grade that has already been awarded. In serious cases, the Rectorate may impose exclusion from studies for up to two semesters.

reranked.title: Maintaining independence: AI-generated or AI-supported?
heading_path: Controlling AI in a targeted manner and maintaining independence
reranked.text:
The quality of AI-generated output depends to a large extent on how precisely and reflectively a request is formulated. AI tools do not independently respond to academic requirements; rather, they orient themselves to the specifications they receive through [prompts](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/controlling-ai-in-a-targeted-manner-the-importance-of-prompting-correctly/).

It is important to define the objective, context and framework conditions clearly, as this will ensure the most effective use of AI as a supportive tool. Through targeted prompting, the user retains control over the working process, determines the direction and depth of responses, and ensures that substantive decisions remain theirs. AI thus provides impulses – the academic contextualisation and further development remain the responsibility of the user.

Formulieren Sie Prompts so, dass die KI **nicht für Sie denkt oder schreibt**, sondern Sie beim eigenen Denken unterstützt. Bitten Sie um **Fragen, Alternativen, Rückmeldungen oder Strukturvorschläge**, nicht um fertige Texte oder inhaltliche Entscheidungen. Bewährt haben sich Prompts, die:

- die KI auf eine **unterstützende Rolle** festlegen (zB Feedback, Reflexionshilfe),
- klar benennen, **was die KI nicht tun soll** (zB keine fertigen Argumente),
- deutlich machen, dass **Auswahl, Bewertung und Weiterverarbeitung** bei Ihnen liegen.

**Dabei gilt:** Die Vorschläge dienen als Ausgangspunkt und müssen fachlich geprüft, eingeordnet und weiterentwickelt werden.

reranked.title: What is permitted? And what is not?
reranked.text:
Whether and how you may use AI in a course or in an examination is determined by the learning outcomes, the fundamental [principles of good academic practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035) and the requirements set by the respective lecturer. Therefore, always check out

- the information in u:find
- notices on Moodle
- information on specific assignments and examinations

In the event of ambiguity surrounding the use of AI, it is advisable to consult with the lecturer in advance to establish clear regulations. In instances where there is uncertainty, it is prudent to assume that AI is not permitted unless explicit permission has been obtained.

AI may be used, provided that responsibility for the content is retained and the independent nature of the work is preserved. For example, AI may be used to:

- generate ideas or structure topics
- gain an overview of a new topic or prepare for reading
- revise the language of one's own texts
- support reflection or check comprehensibility
- find examples or alternatives which are reviewed and contextualised independently

The use of AI must always be compatible with the requirements set by the lecturer and must not replace your independent contribution.

reranked.title: Further information
reranked.text:
Further links with additional information, services and guidelines on dealing with artificial intelligence can be found here.

If you have any questions about the use of AI in courses, please always consult the following first:

- the information in u:find
- notes on Moodle and
- information on specific assignments and examinations

If anything is unclear, please always speak to the course teacher.

There are interdisciplinary courses on AI that are also eligible for ECTS:

- Extension Curriculum ["Understanding Artificial Intelligence"](https://informatik.univie.ac.at/studium/studienangebot/erweiterungscurricula-ec/kuenstliche-intelligenz-verstehen/)
- Courses on AI in [Studienstart Plus](https://studienstartplus.univie.ac.at/)

The [Centre for Teaching and Learning](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/) offers consultations and workshops on AI:

- **[Peer Point](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/student-research-hub/ctl-peer-point/)** for questions about using AI in teaching? Peer Point is here to help you.
- **[Handouts for Students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/ressourcen/handouts-leitfaeden-und-infomaterial/#c1123913)**
- [Schreib-](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/waehrend-des-studiums/schreibberatung/moegliche-themen-einer-schreibberatung/) und Lernberatungen ([PFSB](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/waehrend-des-studiums/peer-studienfortschrittsberatung/))

reranked.title: AI in your Studies: What is permitted?
reranked.text:
AI can provide support at various points throughout your degree programme, such as idea structuring, understanding complex content and text revision. However, there are clear guidelines in place regarding the application and extent to which AI can be utilised in degree programmes.

The University of Vienna supports the responsible use of artificial intelligence in degree programmes, provided that such use is transparent, complies with regulations, upholds the [principles of academic integrity](https://backend.univie.ac.at/?id=225768), and that it supports you in achieving the intended learning outcomes.

Whether and how the use of AI is permitted always depends on the specific context. In case of doubt, the following applies: AI is not permitted unless clear permission has been granted.

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.
